children.text: to preserve and protect for the benefit of present and future generations the nationally significant historic resources associated with the Manhattan Project;
to improve public understanding of the Manhattan Project and the legacy of the Manhattan Project through interpretation of the historic resources associated with the Manhattan Project;
to enhance public access to the Historical Park consistent with protection of public safety, national security, and other aspects of the mission of the Department of Energy; and

to assist the Department of Energy, Historical Park communities, historical societies, and other interested organizations and individuals in efforts to preserve and protect the historically significant resources associated with the Manhattan Project.

children.heading: Historical Park
children.label: 1 Historical Park
children.text: The term “Historical Park” means the Manhattan Project National Historical Park established under subsection (c).

children.heading: Manhattan Project
children.label: 2 Manhattan Project
children.text: The term “Manhattan Project” means the Federal military program to develop an atomic bomb ending on .December 31, 1946

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 3 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Establishment of Manhattan Project National Historical Park
children.label: c Establishment of Manhattan Project National Historical Park

children.heading: Date
children.label: A Date
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , there shall be established as a unit of the National Park System the Manhattan Project National Historical Park.December 19, 2014

children.heading: Areas included
children.label: B Areas included
children.text: The Historical Park shall consist of facilities and areas listed under paragraph (2) as determined by the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Energy. The Secretary shall include the area referred to in paragraph (2)(C)(i), the B Reactor National Historic Landmark, in the Historical Park.

children.label: i
children.text: Buildings 9204–3 and 9731 at the Department of Energy Y–12 National Security Complex;

children.label: ii
children.text: the X–10 Graphite Reactor at the Department of Energy Oak Ridge National Laboratory;

children.label: iii
children.text: the K–25 Building site at the Department of Energy East Tennessee Technology Park;

children.label: iv
children.text: the former Guest House located at 210 East Madison Road; and

children.label: v
children.text: at other sites in Oak Ridge, Tennessee, that are not depicted on the map but are determined by the Secretary to be suitable and appropriate for inclusion in the Historical Park, except that sites administered by the Secretary of Energy may be included only with the concurrence of the Secretary of Energy.

children.label: i
children.text: within the Los Alamos Scientific Laboratory National Historic Landmark District, or any addition to the Landmark District proposed in the National Historic Landmark Nomination—Los Alamos Scientific Laboratory (LASL) NHL District (Working Draft of NHL Revision), Los Alamos National Laboratory document LA–UR 12–00387 ();January 26, 2012

children.label: ii
children.text: the former East Cafeteria located at 1670 Nectar Street; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the former dormitory located at 1725 17th Street.

children.label: i
children.text: the B Reactor National Historic Landmark;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Hanford High School in the town of Hanford and Hanford Construction Camp Historic District;

children.label: iii
children.text: the White Bluffs Bank building in the White Bluffs Historic District;

children.label: iv
children.text: the warehouse at the Bruggemann’s Agricultural Complex;

children.label: v
children.text: the Hanford Irrigation District Pump House; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the T Plant (221–T Process Building).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary and the Secretary of Energy (acting through the Oak Ridge, Los Alamos, and Richland site offices) shall enter into an agreement governing the respective roles of the Secretary and the Secretary of Energy in administering the facilities, land, or interests in land under the administrative jurisdiction of the Department of Energy that is to be included in the Historical Park under subsection (c)(2), including provisions for enhanced public access, management, interpretation, and historic preservation.December 19, 20141So in original. Probably should be “are”.1

children.label: A
children.text: have decisionmaking authority for the content of historic interpretation of the Manhattan Project for purposes of administering the Historical Park; and

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that the agreement provides an appropriate advisory role for the National Park Service in preserving the historic resources covered by the agreement.

children.heading: Responsibilities of the Secretary of Energy
children.label: 3 Responsibilities of the Secretary of Energy
A
children.text: shall ensure that the agreement appropriately protects public safety, national security, and other aspects of the ongoing mission of the Department of Energy at the Oak Ridge Reservation, Los Alamos National Laboratory, and Hanford Site;

children.label: B
children.text: may consult with and provide historical information to the Secretary concerning the Manhattan Project;

children.label: C
children.text: shall retain responsibility, in accordance with applicable law, for any environmental remediation or activities relating to structural safety that may be necessary in or around the facilities, land, or interests in land governed by the agreement; and

children.label: D
children.text: shall retain authority and legal obligations for historic preservation and general maintenance, including to ensure safe access, in connection with the Department’s Manhattan Project resources.

children.heading: Amendments
children.label: 4 Amendments
children.text: The agreement under paragraph (1) may be amended, including to add to the Historical Park facilities, land, or interests in land within the eligible areas described in subsection (c)(2) that are under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Energy.

children.label: A
children.text: before executing any agreement under subsection (d); and

children.label: B
children.text: in the development of the general management plan under subsection (f)(2).

children.heading: Notice of determination
children.label: 2 Notice of determination
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which an agreement under subsection (d) is entered into, the Secretary shall publish in the Federal Register notice of the establishment of the Historical Park, including an official boundary map.

children.heading: Availability of map
children.label: 3 Availability of map
children.text: The official boundary map published under paragraph (2) shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service. The map shall be updated to reflect any additions to the Historical Park from eligible areas described in subsection (c)(2).

children.heading: Additions
children.label: 4 Additions
children.text: Any land, interest in land, or facility within the eligible areas described in subsection (c)(2) that is acquired by the Secretary or included in an amendment to the agreement under subsection (d)(4) shall be added to the Historical Park.

children.label: A
children.text: this section; and

children.label: i
children.text: the National Park System Organic Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 12See References in Text note below.2

children.label: ii
children.text: the Act of ( et seq.).August 21, 193516 U.S.C. 4612

children.heading: General management plan
children.label: 2 General management plan
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the date on which funds are made available to carry out this subsection, the Secretary, with the concurrence of the Secretary of Energy, with respect to land administered by the Secretary of Energy, and in consultation and collaboration with the Oak Ridge, Los Alamos and Richland Department of Energy site offices, shall complete a general management plan for the Historical Park in accordance with .section 100502 of title 54

children.heading: Interpretive tours
children.label: 3 Interpretive tours
children.text: The Secretary may, subject to applicable law, provide interpretive tours of historically significant Manhattan Project sites and resources in the States of Tennessee, New Mexico, and Washington that are located outside the boundary of the Historical Park.

children.label: i
children.text: transfer of administrative jurisdiction from the Department of Energy by agreement between the Secretary and the Secretary of Energy;

children.label: ii
children.text: donation;

children.label: iii
children.text: exchange; or

children.label: iv
children.text: in the case of land and interests in land within the eligible areas described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (c)(2), purchase from a willing seller.

children.heading: No use of condemnation
children.label: B No use of condemnation
children.text: The Secretary may not acquire by condemnation any land or interest in land under this section.

children.heading: Facilities
children.label: C Facilities
children.text: The Secretary may acquire land or interests in land in the vicinity of the Historical Park for visitor and administrative facilities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into one or more agreements with the head of a Federal agency to provide public access to, and management, interpretation, and historic preservation of, historically significant Manhattan Project resources under the jurisdiction or control of the Federal agency.

children.heading: Donations; cooperative agreements
children.label: ii Donations; cooperative agreements
children.text: The Secretary may accept donations from, and enter into cooperative agreements with, State governments, units of local government, tribal governments, organizations, or individuals to further the purpose of an interagency agreement entered into under clause (i) or to provide visitor services and administrative facilities within reasonable proximity to the Historical Park.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: B Technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance to State, local, or tribal governments, organizations, or individuals for the management, interpretation, and historic preservation of historically significant Manhattan Project resources not included within the Historical Park.

children.heading: Donations to Department of Energy
children.label: C Donations to Department of Energy
children.text: For the purposes of this section, or for the purpose of preserving and providing access to historically significant Manhattan Project resources, the Secretary of Energy may accept, hold, administer, and use gifts, bequests, and devises (including labor and services).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Nothing in this section creates a protective perimeter or buffer zone around the boundary of the Historical Park.

children.heading: Activities outside the boundary of the Historical Park
children.label: 2 Activities outside the boundary of the Historical Park
children.text: The fact that an activity or use on land outside the boundary of the Historical Park can be seen or heard from within the boundary shall not preclude the activity or use outside the boundary of the Historical Park.

children.heading: No cause of action
children.label: h No cause of action
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to create a cause of action with respect to activities outside or adjacent to the established boundary of the Historical Park.
